description: Member (age 74, retired) purchased $9,500 in Google Play and Apple gift cards over 3 days after receiving calls from people claiming to be IRS agents and a Social Security Administration officer. Told her SSN was 'compromised in drug trafficking' and she must pay fines immediately or face arrest. Husband alerted when she requested a $4,000 wire.

get_alert.monitoring_rule: RULE-APP-009: Member attempted wire transfer following unusual gift card purchase pattern — branch teller escalation

get_member.address: 441 Bayview Court, Panama City Beach, FL 32407
get_member.cognitive_status_note: Mild cognitive impairment noted in member file — designated POA is Gerald Kowalski

caller_claims: IRS agent badge #4412, claimed her SSN used in drug trafficking case in Texas
Social Security Administration officer — threatened suspension of benefits
Told her she was under investigation and would be arrested if she didn't cooperate
Instructed her NOT to tell family — 'active federal investigation, confidential'

payments.description: Attempted wire — branch teller stopped and escalated

get_triggering_activity.gift_card_redemption: Codes provided to scammers via phone immediately after purchase — all codes redeemed within minutes

get_scam_indicators.caller_id_spoofing: Both numbers confirmed spoofed — actual origin traced to VoIP provider in Nigeria
get_scam_indicators.gift_card_pattern: Rapid sequential pharmacy purchases of high-denomination gift cards — government-impersonation scam signature
get_scam_indicators.code_redemption: All Google Play codes redeemed within 4 minutes of purchase; Apple codes within 7 minutes
vulnerability_factors: Isolation tactic — told not to tell family
Urgency and fear — threat of arrest, benefit suspension
39-year NFCU member — high trust, significant balance
get_scam_indicators.elder_fraud_indicators: Meets Adult Protective Services referral criteria — cognitive impairment combined with financial exploitation

get_recovery_assessment.gift_card_recovery: none — codes were redeemed within minutes, gift card companies cannot reverse
get_recovery_assessment.wire_recovery: n/a — wire was successfully blocked by branch teller
recommended_actions: File police report — financial exploitation of elderly
Refer to Adult Protective Services (Florida DCFS)
Notify POA (Gerald Kowalski) — he is designated
Consider enhanced account protections given cognitive impairment
SAR not required (below $5k threshold per transaction, but consider pattern SAR)

get_reg_e_info.reason: Gift card purchases are not EFTs covered by Reg E. Member voluntarily purchased and provided the codes. Wire was blocked before execution. No unauthorized EFT occurred.
get_reg_e_info.elder_exploitation_note: Florida Statutes §825.103 defines financial exploitation of elderly. APS referral is mandatory for financial institution employees when cognitive impairment is documented.

_meta.design_notes: Government impersonation scam with gift cards instead of wire transfers. Tests: gift card payment type (different from wire-based scams), APS referral requirement, documented cognitive impairment, wire successfully blocked by branch. Reg E doesn't apply. Tests whether system identifies elder exploitation reporting obligations beyond SAR.
